title: Estimating & Breaking Down Tasks
summary: Estimating is predicting how long work will take.
Breaking big tasks into small ones makes estimates better.
Smaller pieces are easier to judge and finish.
Estimates are educated guesses, not promises.

sections.text: Teams need a rough sense of how long work will take, to plan sprints and set expectations. Estimating is that skill, and the single best trick for doing it well is breaking large, fuzzy tasks into small, clear ones.

sections.title: Why Break Things Down
sections.text: A big task like build the user profile page is hard to estimate, because it hides many smaller pieces. Break it into small, specific tasks, and each one becomes far easier to judge. Small pieces are also easier to finish, track, and review, and they reveal hidden work you would have missed in the big version.

sections.title: Estimates Are Guesses
sections.text: An estimate is an educated guess, not a guarantee. Work often surprises you, so good estimates include a little room for the unexpected, and you get better at them with experience. The goal is a useful prediction to plan around, not a promise you will be punished for missing. Honest, thoughtful estimates beat optimistic ones.

media.alt: A big task broken into small pieces, each estimated on its own

sections.title: Planning a Road Trip in Legs
sections.text: Estimating a big task in one go is like guessing a whole road trip's time from a glance at the map. Breaking it into legs, this city to that one, then the next, gives a far more accurate total, and you notice the mountain pass that will slow you down. Small legs add up to a trustworthy estimate.

media.alt: A road trip split into legs, revealing a slow mountain pass

sections.title: Estimating Work
items: Take a big or fuzzy task.
Break it into small, specific pieces.
Estimate each small piece on its own.
Add a little room for the unexpected.
Treat the total as a guess to plan around.

sections.text: Developers are famously optimistic estimators, often assuming everything will go smoothly. Knowing this about yourself, and padding a little, is part of getting better at it.

terms.definition: Predicting how long work will take.

Pieces easier to judge and finish.
